Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Set For Case Management Conference On Aug-26-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Plaintiffs Entire Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-25-2020 Continued To Aug-13-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. MARI BANDOMA CALLADO, Esq. of the Dolan Law Firm, PC (Tel: 415-421-2800) appearing via CourtCall for plaintiff Carol Hass;
Atty. TAMI K. LEE, Esq. of P.K. Schrieffer LLP (Tel: 626-373-2444) appearing via CourtCall for defendants Peter Van Asperen and Kiyomi Van Asperen.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PETER VAN ASPEREN AND KIYOMI VAN ASPEREN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT On Plaintiff's Entire Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel for parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the sole modification to add the word "supplemental" before the word "opposition," as follows: Matter is continued to August 13, 2020. Plaintiff's Code of Civil Procedure 437c(h) request is granted; the declaration of Mari Bandoma Callado demonstrates good cause for the continuance. (See Insalaco v. Hope Lutheran Church of West Contra Costa County (May 27, 2020) 2020 WL 2744095, at *9 [trial court abused its discretion in denying plaintiffs' request for 437c(h) continuance to conduct site inspection to explore "disputed facts that were of critical importance in opposing summary judgment"; under circumstances, continuance was "virtually mandated"].) The SUPPLEMENTAL opposition and reply are due in accordance with the new hearing date in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c. The trial date of August 3, 2020 is vacated.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
